author: ync
title: a review from a year-1 student without any VBA knowledge just having finished COMP1021 in fall
comment_content: Prior to taking this course, I had little knowledge about Excel VBA beyond its connection to Excel and the ability to use programming to apply functions to workbooks. However, this course broadened my understanding of Excel and VBA, equipping me with the skills to tackle more complex problems in the future. The course material covered basic Excel knowledge, functions within Excel cells, and Visual Basic programming.
comment_teaching: I had high expectations for this course, but unfortunately, I was somewhat disappointed with the delivery. While Gibson brought a lot of passion to COMP1021, I didn't feel the same level of engagement in this course. Although I knew that it was an online course, I didn't anticipate that the course materials would consist only of text, screenshots, and files. The absence of video explanations made it difficult for me to understand certain concepts at times. Furthermore, the only means of communication with Gibson and the TA was through email, which was not always convenient. However, I found that they were friendly and responsive in answering questions. I would have appreciated a Zoom session for inquiries.
comment_grading: Overall, the grading for this course is reasonable. To pass the course, you need to score at least 60% on the exercises and 40% on the final exam. There are four exercises in total, and they are not overly difficult. You only need to complete three of them and score well to pass the course. The final exam is a one-hour exam with 20 questions, most of which are straightforward, although a few are a bit tricky. It is relatively easy to score 40% or higher on the exam.
comment_workload:
The workload for this course depends on your goals.
If you want to take it easy, the workload is relatively small.
However, if you want to ensure that you have a thorough understanding of the course materials, the workload can be quite substantial. COMP1021 is a 3-credit course with 7 labs, so one might expect that COMP1029V, which is a 1-credit course, would have 2-3 exercises. Unfortunately, there are 4 exercises. Within the same semester, I also took COMP1029C, so I felt like I was constantly fighting against deadlines. It took me about 30 minutes to read, take notes, and complete the quiz (which doesn't count towards the grade) for each section of a lesson, but it took me hours to complete each exercise.

author: don't give up
title: Free credit for SBM students?
comment_content:
Just basic Excel and VBA stuff. For SBM students, most (~70%) of the stuff is covered in ISOM2010 lab, so it's almost like 1 free credit if you haven't forgotten the stuff yet.

Also one weird thing I found is that the Excel part doesn't cover VLOOKUP or XLOOKUP, which I think are popular formulas.

Bonus: One of the assignments is making a game in Excel, really interesting
comment_teaching: I didn't put Gibson as the instructor as we couldn't see him for once throughout the course (except in the final exam). The only teaching material is reading on Canvas, and there's no teaching video or stuff.
comment_grading:
Passing criteria: 60% (240/400) for 4 assignments + 40% for final exam (open book).
What I can say is that the final exam is somewhat similar to the sample paper provided, you won't fail if you can handle the sample paper.
comment_workload: 4 assignments in total (you can choose to do 3 for passing), 1 per month in a regular semester. Not time consuming at all, easier than 1021/2011 labs.

comment_content: We will be learning some excel basic cell coding, macros, and VBA programming in this course. This 1 credit course won't teach you everything, but is a very good bridging course.
comment_teaching: The teaching is online and self-paced. The professor have several modules of content prepared for you in canvas, alongside with ungraded quizzes to test your knowledge. But the only thing that I don't like about this course is that the professor will lock up modules beyond module 2, and only release a new one after 3 weeks, so it is not completely self-paced, you need to spare a few hours on learning new stuff and finishing the assignment every 3 weeks.
comment_grading: It's a p/f course, very easy to pass you only need to get average 60% in assignments and 40% in final exam. The last requirement is to submit at least 3 out of 4 assignments, if you do some quick maths you will know this is more like a joke than a rule.
comment_workload: I would say moderate workload as a 1 credit course, 4 assignments but you only need to get 60% on average to pass. (The first 2 assignments are easy, I got 195% in total of these two and only needed to get 45% in total for the remaining 2 harder assignments. So remember not to score too low on the first 2 assignments or your life will be harder. For the exam, you need to score 40%, very easy to achieve if you have done the past paper he gives you. (You can even bring a cheatsheet to the exam, I brought nothing and still easily got 7X%, I suggest you to print out the answers of the past paper into the final if you really need to)

Topics included:
1️⃣ Introduction to Excel
      🔶 Getting Started
      🔶 Introduction to Spreadsheets
      🔶 Cell References

Pass or fail.

Need to get above 60% overall in the assignments (by finishing at least 3 out of 4 of them) and 40% in the final exam. These should be a piece of cake as soon as you pay attention to the deadlines and revise for the exam.

author: lmacchch
title: Don't be fooled by ustspace. VBA is a programming language which is quite different from other languages
comment_content: There are lots of contents on Canvas which you are able to learn by yourself. I strongly recommend you to learn them seriously and carefully, especially for the first two parts of the contents, as a good understanding of those basic contents is essential for you to be succeed in the exam
comment_teaching: As a self-study course, there is no teaching in the course at all.
comment_grading:
P/F
You need to get 240/400 in exercise and 40/100 in exam to pass the course.
My scores: exercise1: 100/100 exercise2: 100/100 exercise3:70/100 exercise4: 100/100
Even though I get good scores in exercise, the exam in this year is extremely difficult. There are 20 exercises in total in the paper, with lots of small blanks. It's extremely difficult for you to finish them within one hour. Therefore, you should get good scores in the straight forward questions or you will possibly fail the course.
I didn't learn those basic knowledge about VBA well, so I got great panic during the exam .Even though I got A in COMP1021, due to the great panic, I almost finished nothing about the logic-required part of the exam. And due to my weak master of the basic knowledge, I almost fail the course.
My advice is: don't be fooled by ustspace, you need to learn that seriously or you will fail!!
comment_workload: Little workload. It's easy to finish the required exercise. However, if you want to truly master VBA, you do really need lots of effort.
